title: Consolidating Duplicates
concept: Refactoring repetitious code sections into shared utilities or modules to improve code hygiene.

exercises.prompt: What should you do with code that is no longer used or needed?

options.text: Delete it to improve code clarity
Comment it out for future reference

exercises.explain: Deleting unused code helps keep your code clean and understandable.

cards.title: What is Consolidating Duplicates?
cards.body: Consolidating duplicates means taking code that is repeated in different places and making a single version of it to use everywhere. This makes the code easier to manage and understand.

cards.title: Definition
cards.body: Refactoring repetitious code sections into shared utilities or modules helps improve code hygiene by reducing redundancy.

cards.title: Cleaning Up Your Room
cards.body: Imagine you have several toys scattered everywhere. If you put all the same types of toys in one box, it'll be easier to find them. Similarly, consolidating code makes it simpler to maintain.

cards.title: Example of Consolidating Duplicates
cards.body: Instead of writing the same function to calculate the area of a rectangle in multiple places, create one function that all parts of your program can use.

cards.title: Myth: More Code Means More Control
cards.body: Some believe that having more code gives more flexibility, but it often creates confusion. Clean code is about clarity, not quantity!

cards.title: Visual Comparison
cards.body: See how duplicate code can be streamlined?

left.text:
function calculateArea(length, width) { return length * width; } 
function calculateArea(length, width) { return length * width; }

right.text:
function calculateArea(length, width) { return length * width; } 
let area = calculateArea(5, 10);

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of consolidating duplicate code?

options.text: To make the code more complicated
To reduce repetition and improve clarity
To increase the size of the codebase

exercises.explain: Reducing repetition makes the code clearer and easier to manage.

exercises.prompt: Consolidating duplicates in code can help improve maintenance and understanding. True or False?
exercises.explain: True! Consolidating duplicates enhances code maintenance and understanding.

exercises.prompt: When we consolidate duplicates, we refactor repetitive code sections into shared _______.
exercises.sentence: When we consolidate duplicates, we refactor repetitive code sections into shared utilities.
exercises.explain: Utilities are shared pieces of code that help avoid repetition and improve clarity.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements is generated by the AI?

options.text: Cleaning code is important for software efficiency.
Consolidating duplicates should be avoided to retain individual function clarity.
Refactoring repeated code into common utilities helps streamline development.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated statement highlights the benefit of refactoring for efficiency and clarity.

exercises.prompt: Why is it beneficial to consolidate duplicate code into a single module?

options.text: It makes the codebase larger and harder to navigate.
It decreases readability and increases mistakes.
It simplifies code management and improves maintenance, making it easier to update in one place.
It encourages more code duplication in the project.

exercises.explain: Consolidating duplicate code improves overall code hygiene, as changes only need to be made in one location, thus reducing the risk of errors and maintenance overhead.
